examples.title: CP System Behavior Simulation
examples.description: Demonstrates how a CP system prioritizes consistency over availability during partition
examples.explanation: Demonstrates CP system behavior: During network partition, both reads and writes fail to maintain consistency. The system becomes unavailable rather than risk returning stale data.

examples.title: AP System Behavior Simulation
examples.description: Demonstrates how an AP system prioritizes availability over consistency during partition
examples.code:
import time
from enum import Enum
from datetime import datetime

class APDatabase:
 """AP System - Prioritizes Availability over Consistency."""
 
 def __init__(self):
 # Each node has its own data (can diverge during partition)
 self.node_a_data = {}
 self.node_b_data = {}
 self.partitioned = False
 self.pending_sync = [] # Track writes that need syncing
 
 def write(self, key, value, node='A'):
 """Write always succeeds (to local node)."""
 timestamp = datetime.now().isoformat()
 
 if node == 'A':
 self.node_a_data[key] = {'value': value, 'ts': timestamp}
 else:
 self.node_b_data[key] = {'value': value, 'ts': timestamp}
 
 if self.partitioned:
 # Queue for later sync
 self.pending_sync.append((key, value, timestamp, node))
 return f"Write to {node}: {key}={value} (pending sync)"
 else:
 # Sync immediately to other node
 self._sync_write(key, value, timestamp)
 return f"Write synced: {key}={value}"
 
 def _sync_write(self, key, value, timestamp):
 """Sync write to both nodes."""
 data = {'value': value, 'ts': timestamp}
 self.node_a_data[key] = data
 self.node_b_data[key] = data
 
 def read(self, key, node='A'):
 """Read always returns (may be stale)."""
 data = self.node_a_data if node == 'A' else self.node_b_data
 result = data.get(key)
 if result:
 stale = " (possibly stale)" if self.partitioned else ""
 return f"{result['value']}{stale}"
 return None
 
 def simulate_partition(self):
 self.partitioned = True
 print("NETWORK PARTITION! Nodes can diverge.")
 
 def heal_partition(self):
 """Heal partition and resolve conflicts (last-write-wins)."""
 self.partitioned = False
 print("Healing partition... resolving conflicts...")
 
 # Merge data using last-write-wins
 all_keys = set(self.node_a_data.keys()) | set(self.node_b_data.keys())
 for key in all_keys:
 a_data = self.node_a_data.get(key, {'ts': '0'})
 b_data = self.node_b_data.get(key, {'ts': '0'})
 
 # Last write wins
 if a_data['ts'] > b_data['ts']:
 self.node_b_data[key] = a_data
 else:
 self.node_a_data[key] = b_data
 
 print("Partition healed, data synchronized")

# Demo AP System
print("=== AP System Demo ===")
ap_db = APDatabase()

# Normal operation
print(ap_db.write("item:1", "Apple"))
print(f"Read from A: {ap_db.read('item:1', 'A')}")
print(f"Read from B: {ap_db.read('item:1', 'B')}")

# During partition - writes still work!
ap_db.simulate_partition()
print(ap_db.write("item:1", "Banana", node='A')) # Node A writes
print(ap_db.write("item:1", "Cherry", node='B')) # Node B writes different value!

print(f"Read from A: {ap_db.read('item:1', 'A')}") # Banana
print(f"Read from B: {ap_db.read('item:1', 'B')}") # Cherry (inconsistent!)

# Heal and resolve
ap_db.heal_partition()
print(f"After healing - A: {ap_db.read('item:1', 'A')}")
print(f"After healing - B: {ap_db.read('item:1', 'B')}")
examples.explanation: Demonstrates AP system behavior: During partition, both reads and writes continue to work, but nodes can have inconsistent data. After partition heals, conflicts are resolved (using last-write-wins in this example).

examples.title: Tunable Consistency (Cassandra-style)
examples.description: Demonstrates tunable consistency levels like in Apache Cassandra
examples.explanation: Shows how systems like Cassandra allow tunable consistency. ONE gives highest availability, ALL gives strongest consistency, QUORUM provides balance.
examples.timeComplexity: O(r) where r is required replicas
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n * k) for n replicas and k keys
